auth_notes: Requires Autodesk Developer account to generate Client ID and Client Secret. Implementation in n8n requires manual token management or configuring the OAuth2 credentials type with specific scopes (data:read, data:write, bucket:create, etc.).
rate_limits: Varies by service; Data Management API typically allows 300-500 requests per minute. Model Derivative API uses a credit-based system for translations.
gotchas: Large CAD files require chunked/resumable upload logic which is difficult to implement in standard HTTP nodes.
File translation (Model Derivative API) is asynchronous; you must implement a polling loop or a webhook listener to detect when a file is ready.
URNs for files must be Base64 encoded (without padding) before being passed to certain endpoints.
operations_available: Upload source CAD files (DWG, STEP, RVT, etc.)
Trigger Model Derivative translation (convert to SVF/OBJ/STL)
Extract metadata and Bill of Materials (BOM)
Generate signed URLs for temporary file access
research_notes: Since CAD/CAM is a category, this analysis focuses on Autodesk Platform Services (formerly Forge) as it is the industry standard for CAD/CAM API integrations. Integration in n8n will require the HTTP Request node for all calls, specifically handling binary data for file transfers and complex JSON bodies for translation jobs.
